summary: Iter49 backend refactor regression verified. server.py split into core.py/models.py/seed_data.py/routes/*.py behaves identically. 22/22 pytest cases pass (core, restaurants, cuisines sorted, spin, places search+photo allowlist 404, stats fates/crawls + increments, crawls create/get/422 for 1 stop, admin login bad/good, verify, sponsors GET, sponsors/stats mrr+price=29, submissions, all auth-required endpoints return 401 without Bearer, full sponsor CRUD POST->PATCH->DELETE). Frontend Home loads (Fork·Fate title), header buttons (Guided/Sponsor your spot/Favorites/Download the app/Add spot) render, category tabs Food/Drinks/Bars/Desserts switch (Desserts -> 'Let fate pick your sweet treat'), Deal button triggers shuffle animation and reveals a Fate card ('Flour & Butter Bakery'), 0 console errors from homeConstants.js extraction. Admin /admin login with GrimReaper!2026 succeeds, MRR ($0.00, $0/yr), PayingSubs ($29/mo each), sponsor list ('Current sponsors (0)') all render, 0 console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py refactor is clean: thin entrypoint (54 lines) wiring routers + CORS + startup/shutdown. core.py centralizes DB, auth, rate-limit, helpers.
core.py rate-limit uses in-memory buckets (defaultdict(deque)) which is fine for single-worker but will not share across replicas if the app is scaled horizontally.
GOOGLE_SEARCH_DAILY_CAP counter (_GOOGLE_DAY) is also in-memory only — will reset on backend restart; acceptable for preview but worth noting for prod.
app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI ≥ 0.109 in favor of lifespan handlers; still works but consider migrating.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_ crawl (2 stops, 'TEST_Iter49 Crawl') and one TEST_ sponsor (created then deleted in CRUD test). Stats counters incremented by 1 each for /stats/fate-dealt and /stats/crawl-completed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend refactor into modules is fully green. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter49_refactor.py as a smoke regression suite. Did NOT hit any live PayPal endpoints (checkout not exercised). Google Places search returned source='curated' (fallback), as expected in preview.
